round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent had no Round 1 evidence to improve upon; correctly remained silent.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent acknowledged other agents' findings (Lombardy Poplars) and refined confidence, though the primary evidence was already present in R1.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent explicitly integrated regulatory and landscape evidence to strengthen the botanical argument, showing synthesis.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent incorporated meta-agent observations (fence posts) to refine the regulatory assessment.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent integrated botanical and landscape evidence to reinforce the meta-level conclusion.

role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced 'chip-seal road surfaces and left-hand traffic' (regulatory/landscape) to support botanical claims.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced 'dense temperate rainforest vegetation' (landscape/botanical) to support regulatory claims.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Minor leakage: Referenced 'Tree ferns and Lombardy poplars' (botanical) to support meta-level image quality claims.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the evidence, noting the absence of linguistic data and effectively weighting the strong convergence of botanical (tree ferns), landscape (poplars), and regulatory (LHT, chip-seal) evidence to confirm New Zealand. The reasoning was clear and eliminated alternatives logically.
